era_context: The early 10th century CE continues to be characterized by political fragmentation and reorganization across Eurasia. The Islamic world is experiencing the rise of regional dynasties challenging Abbasid authority. In Europe, feudalism is consolidating, and the Byzantine Empire is in a period of military and cultural revival under Constantine VII. East Asia remains fragmented among competing regional powers. This is a transitional era between classical empires and medieval states.

events.title: Constantine VII Porphyrogennetos becomes sole emperor of Byzantine Empire
events.description: Constantine VII Porphyrogennetos, born in the purple (porphyrogennetos), formally became the sole emperor of the Byzantine Empire in 913, though he had been nominal emperor since 912. His reign (913–959) is marked by the regency of his mother Zoe Karbonopsina and later by his own rule. Constantine VII is remembered as a scholar and patron of the arts, and his reign coincided with the Byzantine Macedonian Renaissance, a period of cultural and military revival. He commissioned important works on imperial administration and history.
key_figures: Zoe Karbonopsina (regent, mother of Constantine VII)

sources.name: Constantine VII, De Administrando Imperio (On the Administration of the Empire)

events.certainty_note: Constantine VII's accession to sole imperial authority in 913 is well-documented in Byzantine sources. The date is established with high confidence.
cross_references: Precedes Constantine VII's cultural and military achievements (913–959)
Part of Byzantine Macedonian Renaissance (9th–11th centuries)

events.title: Later Liang Dynasty under Emperor Taizong continues to rule central China
events.description: Emperor Taizong of Later Liang (r. 912–923) continued to rule the central plains of China. In 913, the Later Liang Dynasty was engaged in military conflicts with neighboring kingdoms, particularly the Northern Han and the Southern Tang. The Later Liang Dynasty was characterized by military strength but also internal instability.
key_figures: Zhu You/Emperor Taizong of Later Liang (r. 912–923)

sources.name: Zizhi Tongjian (Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government)
Official histories of the Five Dynasties

events.certainty_note: The Later Liang Dynasty's rule in 913 is well-documented in Chinese historical sources.
cross_references: Part of Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period (907–960)

events.title: Umayyad Caliphate of al-Andalus under Abd al-Rahman III continues consolidation
events.description: Abd al-Rahman III continued to consolidate and expand Umayyad power in al-Andalus. In 913, he was in the process of strengthening the Umayyad state and establishing it as a major Mediterranean power. His reign (912–961) represented a golden age of Umayyad rule in Iberia.
events.certainty_note: Abd al-Rahman III's reign and general consolidation of power are well-documented, but specific events of 913 are less precisely dated.

events.title: Fatimid Caliphate consolidates control in North Africa
events.description: The Fatimid Caliphate, recently proclaimed (909/916), continued to consolidate its control over North Africa. The Fatimids were expanding their territorial control and establishing administrative structures during this period.
events.certainty_note: The Fatimid consolidation in North Africa is well-documented in general terms, but specific events of 913 are less precisely dated.

disconfirming_evidence: Some sources place Constantine VII's assumption of sole imperial authority in 912 rather than 913, creating minor chronological ambiguity. However, 913 is the more commonly cited date in modern scholarship. No major disconfirming evidence undermines the other events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 913 CE is moderate in quality. Byzantine sources are relatively contemporary and detailed, particularly for Constantine VII's reign. Chinese sources provide good documentation of the Five Dynasties period. Islamic sources are available but are later compilations. The surviving sources emphasize political and military events; economic, social, and cultural developments are underrepresented. Sub-Saharan Africa and much of Northern Europe remain poorly documented.

graph_edges.note: Constantine VII's accession in 913 led to his long reign and cultural achievements until 959
Later Liang rule during Five Dynasties period eventually ended with Song unification
